_sources.discrepancies: No major discrepancies found. Maine uses ranked-choice voting for federal and statewide offices. 2026 registration deadlines estimated based on Maine statute (15 business days before election); TODO verify exact 2026 dates. NCSL classifies Maine as no-document-required for ID.

earlyVoting.notes: Absentee voting available; in-person absentee starts ~30 days before Election Day. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Absentee voting (in-person and by mail) available from approximately 30 days before Election Day. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Maine offers in-person absentee voting at town/city clerks' offices beginning approximately 30 days before the election. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Maine law does not prohibit phones inside polling places. Photography of other voters without consent is not permitted. Electioneering is prohibited within 250 feet of the polling place entrance.
additionalRules: Maine uses ranked-choice voting (RCV) for federal offices (U.S. Senate, U.S. House) and statewide offices (Governor, etc.). Voters rank candidates in order of preference.
Same-day voter registration is available; bring proof of identity and residency.
Undeclared (unenrolled) voters may request a party primary ballot on Election Day.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 3 business days before the election (received by town/city clerk)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the town/city clerk by the close of polls on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the clerk's office by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
